title: Connector inbound messages have no real ordering (description + comments share now())
description: Inbound sync inserts description + all comments via sync_inbound_external_comment which uses created_at DEFAULT now(); in one reconcile tx they all get the identical transaction-start now(), and the thread sorts ORDER BY created_at ASC, id ASC (id = random gen_random_uuid()). So description vs comments (and comments among themselves) sort randomly, not chronologically. Fix: thread real timestamps through the DEFINER — add p_created_at (issue created time for the description, each comment's real CreatedAt for comments). Needs a migration (DEFINER signature change → DROP+CREATE; check source pins) + FetchIssue to fetch the issue 'created' field + ExternalIssue.CreatedAt. Surfaced reviewing jira-sync-description.
close_reason: Fixed 2026-06-19: sync_inbound_external_comment gains p_created_at (migration 0068, DROP+CREATE); jira FetchIssue parses issue 'created' → ExternalIssue.CreatedAt; inbound_sync threads issue created time (description) + each comment CreatedAt; NULL→COALESCE now() fallback. 4 source pins updated to 5-param. Integration test pins chronological order; jira unit test pins created parse. Commit add3b52.

title: Connector: trigger agent re-triage on new external comments?
description: Follow-up to manyforge-edq. That fix makes connector-sourced NEW tickets auto-trigger agents (emits ticket.created from sync_inbound_external_issue on the CREATE path only). OPEN PRODUCT QUESTION: should a NEW inbound external COMMENT/reply on an existing connector ticket also (re-)trigger an agent run, mirroring the native-inbox reply re-triage (internal/agents reply_trigger / TopicMessageReceived)? Currently sync_inbound_external_comment appends the message but emits no event, so agents do not re-engage on connector replies. Decide: (a) leave as-is (creation-only), (b) emit a message.received-style event from sync_inbound_external_comment on a genuinely-new comment (dedupe-aware: it already returns NULL on dup) and wire the connector path into the existing reply re-triage loop-guard. Needs the same loop-guard discipline as native (do not re-trigger on the agent's own outbound connector comment).
close_reason: Shipped 2026-06-19: inbound subscriber emits message.received for a comment synced onto an already-existing connector ticket (waking the opt-in, hourly-capped ReplyRetriageTrigger); first-import backlog suppressed via connector_ticket_exists DEFINER (migration 0069). Loop-safe via existing external_id dedup (agent's own echoed-back reply is deduped, emits nothing). Product decision: build w/ backlog suppression (user-confirmed). Integration-tested. Commit 81c33da.

title: Connector inbound sync latency / confirm dev poller is live
description: User report: Jira ticket 'didn't sync until I hit Sync'. Investigation: Reconciler auto-polls every 1m (cmd/manyforge/main.go:324 Every=time.Minute, StaleAfter=5m; started main.go:552-554) + webhook endpoint registered (main.go:763). Manual Sync (POST /{cid}/sync → ReconcileOne) bypasses the poll window. Likely just ≤60s poll latency = by-design. TODO: confirm the reconciler is actually running + authed in dev (if a ticket never auto-appears after >1m, the poller may be failing silently — check logs / connector health). Lower priority than the agent-trigger gap.
notes: RESOLVED + user-confirmed live. Real root cause was NOT latency: jira ListUpdatedSince formatted the incremental 'since' as a UTC absolute, but Jira reads bare JQL datetimes in the ACCOUNT timezone → for sysward.atlassian.net (US, behind UTC) the window landed in the future → incremental sweep returned nothing; only full-pull Sync worked. Fix (6905aad): TZ-independent relative JQL (updated >= -Nm) + regression tests. StaleAfter 5m→1m (ee9af1c) kept as a latency bonus. User confirmed '1 is fixed'.

title: Connector Test verifier: live credential probe (Jira /myself, Zendesk /users/me)
description: The connector Test action returns 'verification unavailable' because connSvc.Verify is nil (main.go:293) and no concrete Verifier exists (deferred in types.go). Add a lightweight authenticated probe to each connector client, a registry-backed connectors.Verifier, and wire connSvc.Verify in main.go so the Test button does a real credential check. Surfaced live by the user 2026-06-14.

title: Connector clients discard the underlying transport error (log real cause before collapsing to ErrUnreachable)
description:
When httpClient.Do fails, jira/client.go:372 and zendesk/client.go:92 return fmt.Errorf("...: transport: %w", ErrUnreachable) and DISCARD the real underlying error entirely — it is not even logged server-side. A network failure, a TLS error, a DNS failure, a context timeout, and a netsafe SSRF dial-refusal all collapse into the identical opaque 'service unreachable'.

Impact: on 2026-06-12 a Little Snitch firewall block of the backend's outbound connections to Jira presented identically to a genuine upstream outage — every reconcile/outbound op logged only 'jira: transport: jira: service unreachable' with no cause. curl from the same host worked (curl had an allow rule; each Go binary is judged separately), but the logs gave zero signal pointing at the firewall. Cost a full multi-step investigation (probe binary replicating the netsafe dialer) to surface what a single log line would have shown.

Fix: before collapsing to the generic ErrUnreachable sentinel (which must STILL be what callers see — Principle II: never leak upstream/library internals to clients), log the real err server-side at WARN with enough context (connector_id, host, err). Mirrors the existing CLAUDE.md error-handling rule: 'Log the wrapped error server-side; return a stable error code + generic message.' Same treatment for the build-request branches (361 / 80) which also drop the cause. Consider including err.Error() only in the server log, never in the returned error.

Also see jira/client.go:394 (upstream status -> ErrUnreachable) which similarly hides the status code from logs.

Files: internal/connectors/jira/client.go, internal/connectors/zendesk/client.go. No behavior change for callers; logging-only + test that asserts the underlying cause is logged (e.g. a fake RoundTripper returning a sentinel error, assert it appears in a captured slog handler).
close_reason: jira+zendesk clients now log the real transport/build-request cause at WARN (method + query-stripped URL + err) before collapsing to ErrUnreachable; caller still sees only the sentinel (Principle II). In-package tests pin cause-logged + cause-not-leaked. test/sec-test/lint green.

title: Connectors: auto-re-adopt detached tickets on reconnect
description: Deferred from manyforge-4zs.3. On hard-delete, a connector's tickets are detached to native with external_id/external_url PRESERVED. When a new connector is later created for the same (business,type,base_url), offer to re-adopt those orphaned tickets by matching external_url host + relinking by external_id, instead of re-importing them as duplicates. v1 does not auto-relink.
close_reason: Auto-re-adopt detached tickets + their messages on connector create: newest-per-external_id wins (dups stay detached), host match via split_part(external_url,'/',3), messages gated on external_id (CHECK), connector.tickets_readopted audit with readopted+skipped counts. Same-tx/atomic, no DEFINER. 4 integration cases + source pin; build/test/sec-test/lint green. Spec+plan in docs/superpowers.

title: db/schema.sql missing ticket_message_source_approval_idx (mirror drift from migration 0030)
description: Opus US6 review finding (non-blocking): db/schema.sql (the sqlc schema source) lacks the partial UNIQUE index ticket_message_source_approval_idx that migration 0030 creates. Harmless today — sqlc parses the ON CONFLICT (source_approval_item_id) WHERE ... arbiter syntactically without validating against an index, and the test/runtime DB built from migrations has the index — but schema.sql has drifted from migrations. Reconcile so schema.sql stays an accurate mirror. Predates US6 (column+index landed in 0030).

title: Add absolute page cap to ListUpdatedSince pagination (Jira + Zendesk)
description: US5 close-gate review (opus) observation 2: ListUpdatedSince in both internal/connectors/zendesk/client.go (terminates on empty page or len(ids)>=Count) and the Jira sibling (startAt>=Total) could in principle loop if a pathological/malicious upstream returns non-empty pages while perpetually inflating Count/Total. Parity behavior, no regression, but add an absolute max-pages guard (e.g. cap at N pages then stop + log) as defense-in-depth against an upstream that never converges. Affects both connectors.

title: Gate connector base_url http:// scheme behind allow_private_base_url trust flag
description: US5 close-gate review (opus) observation 1: validateBaseURL (internal/connectors/credential.go) accepts http:// as well as https:// for ALL connectors (Jira + Zendesk). Load-bearing SSRF control is netsafe dial-time IP refusal, which blocks private/loopback/metadata regardless of scheme, so this is defense-in-depth only, not a vuln. On-prem connectors may legitimately need http, which is exactly the case the allow_private_base_url trust flag already gates. Decide+implement: permit http:// ONLY when allow_private_base_url=true; require https:// otherwise. Framework-wide (affects Jira too), not US5-specific.

title: Generalize inbound conflict audit beyond status to priority/subject
description: Migration 0046's sync_inbound_external_issue conflict-detection block (connector.conflict.resolved / external_wins) only covers the status scalar. Extend both-changed detection + audit payload to priority and subject divergence. Out of scope for US4's demonstrative status pin (T6).

title: Decide native-email suppression for connector-linked ticket replies
description: US4 outbound dispatch is ADDITIVE — the ticket.replied email is still enqueued alongside the outbound Jira comment op for connector-linked tickets (double-notify). Decide whether to suppress the native email when a reply is mirrored to the external connector (single-channel) vs keep both. Product/UX call with regression risk to the email path's tests. See plan D2 + US4 close review (a7j.4).

title: US8 — Multi-provider coverage: per-credential self-host trust + Ollama/vLLM e2e fixtures + SSRF/cost pins
description: Spec 003 US8 (P3). US1 already shipped the openaicompat transport (OpenAI/Ollama/vLLM), netsafe SSRF client wired into the factory, SSRF pins, and golden-fixture round-trips; US7 added the model_pricing catalog. US8 closes 4 gaps: (1) per-credential allow_private_base_url trust flag (migration 0039) so a self-hosted Ollama/vLLM on loopback/RFC1918 is reachable — netsafe gains an AllowPrivate option with metadata IPs hard-blocked even when trusted; validated + audited at create time. (2) Recorded text+tool_call fixtures per provider (ollama, vllm) + an end-to-end test driving credential->Resolve->factory->transport per provider. (3) Lock unknown-self-host-model -> cost 0 run-proceeds (already the behavior at main.go:169) with a debug log + regression pin. (4) Security pins: metadata blocked even with trust on, create-time + dial-time refusal when trust off, hatch works when on, source-level netsafe wiring. No OpenAPI change (no credential HTTP surface). Design: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-06-us8-multi-provider-design.md

title: US7 follow-up: handler-level unit tests for listRuns + getSummary
description: The HTTP-mapping layer for the two new accounting read endpoints lacks unit tests: listRuns (limit clamp, cursor passthrough, next_cursor serialization, window-error->400) and getSummary/toSummaryResp (budget_pct only on this_month, omitted otherwise). Behavior is covered by the store integration test + the Playwright e2e, but the thin HTTP glue is untested. From US7 final review.

title: US6 follow-up: per-tool MCP reclassification (Safe/Reversible auto-exec)
description: v1 classifies ALL MCP tools as Effect=External (fail-closed -> approval). Allow per-tool reclassification so an admin can mark specific MCP tools Safe/Reversible for auto-exec (mode-dependent), like internal tools. Requires a per-tool policy store keyed by mcp:<server>:<tool> + UI. Spec 003 US6 §5.

title: US6 follow-up: DB-audit per-server MCP discovery failures (not just slog)
description: Design 3.3/4 say a per-server MCP discovery failure is 'audited agent.mcp.discovery_failed'. Current impl (mcp_host.go DiscoverTools) LOGS it via slog.WarnContext with that event name and fails open; only a resolver-level (DB-down) failure emits a DB audit row. Task 11 plan text explicitly permitted '(or log)', so v1 ships with slog. If full audit-trail parity is later required (SOC/repudiation), thread the engine's DBAuditor into MCPHost and emit a per-failed-server agent.mcp.discovery_failed audit row alongside the log. LOW priority — fail-open operational event, no security-tamper concern.
close_reason: DiscoverTools returns []DiscoveryFailure; engine emits a per-server agent.mcp.discovery_failed audit row (parity with resolver-level failure), still fail-open + logged. Unit + integration (audit-row) tests green.

title: US6 follow-up: MCP executor should split transport-error (reschedule) from result-isError (complete)
description: Task 9 review (US6): ApprovalExecutor.InvokeMCPTool currently maps an MCP tools/call result with isError=true to a Go error, which causes the approval to reschedule and re-dispatch -> double-fire of a foreign side effect that already happened on the server. A returned response (even isError) means the server processed the request; it should be treated as COMPLETED (MarkExecuted + feed the error result to the model, no reschedule). Only TRANSPORT errors (no response received) should reschedule. This is consistent with the at-least-once contract (design 3.6) today and only matters when the remote tool returns a business-level error after performing a side effect. Pair this with the terminal approval 'failed' state work in manyforge-sa8 so failure handling (transport vs result-error vs terminal-fail) is unified across internal and MCP tools.
close_reason: InvokeMCPTool now returns (out, toolErr, err): transport err reschedules; isError result completes (toolErr=true, content fed back), no reschedule/double-fire. Executor audits completed tool-error as executed:error. Host + executor unit tests pin both sides; old isError->error test inverted. test/sec-test/lint green.

title: US5 follow-up: optional re-triage on message.received (customer reply) with its own loop-guard
description: US5 deliberately triggers only on ticket.created (the structural loop-guard). Re-triaging on a customer's later reply (message.received) needs a dedicated guard so an agent-provoked inbound never re-triggers. Scope + design separately. Also a related wart: a queued run whose agent is deleted before drain stays queued (the claim JOINs agent, so it's never claimed) — decide expire/cleanup with manyforge-sa8 (terminal failed state).

title: US4 follow-up: surface approval_item failure reason + terminal 'failed' state
description: The approval_item.error column (mig 0030) and ApprovalItem.Error domain field are read but never written — the ApprovalExecutor records tool-invoke failures + unknown-tool to the audit log only. A dead-lettered approved item (outbox MaxAttempts exhausted) stays 'approved' forever with no operator-visible failure reason or terminal 'failed' state. Either: (a) populate error + add a 'failed' state the executor sets on permanent failure (operator visibility), or (b) drop the unused error column. Surfaced by US4 final holistic review. Non-blocking.
close_reason: Added terminal 'failed' approval state (mig 0051) + MarkApprovalFailed. Executor marks unknown-tool/no-host and final-attempt-exhaustion as 'failed' with reason; transient non-final still reschedules; completed tool-error stays executed. Event.MaxAttempts stamped by worker for final-attempt detection. Unit + integration tests green.

title: US3 follow-up: promote agent RunLimits + temperature to config keys
description: Loop bounds (max_iterations=8 / max_tokens_per_run=100k / max_output_tokens=4096 / wall_clock=120s) + temperature=0.0 ship as code defaults in internal/agents/runner.go (RunLimits + defaultTemperature), injected into the Engine in cmd/manyforge/main.go via RunLimits{}. Design §8 wants config keys. Wire them through the project config system and pass into the Engine at construction. Also consider hard concurrent-budget enforcement here (US3's mid-run guard reads month-to-date once and is best-effort/soft under concurrent runs — documented in runner.go).
close_reason: RunLimits (max_iterations/tokens_per_run/output_tokens/wall_clock) + temperature now load from MANYFORGE_AGENT_* config keys (defaults = code defaults), wired into Engine in main.go. RunLimits.Temperature field added; loop uses it. config_test + .env.example added. Concurrent-budget hard-enforcement left as separate concern.

title: US3: decide AI Request sampling-default handling (temperature unset vs explicit-0)
description:
US1b transports serialize Request.Temperature as-is. Because ai.Request.Temperature is a non-pointer float64, the transport cannot distinguish 'unset' from 'explicit 0', so an unset request sends temperature:0 (deterministic) to both Anthropic and OpenAI rather than the provider's natural default (~1.0). For a tool-calling agent runtime, deterministic-by-default is defensible, but it's currently implicit.

US3 decision when wiring agent definitions: either (a) make ai.Request.Temperature a *float64 (pointer; nil=provider default) — a US1a schema change rippling through both transports + mock, or (b) add ',omitempty' to the wire Temperature tags (unset 0 dropped → provider default; loses ability to force 0), or (c) keep current behavior and have agent definitions always set Temperature explicitly. Recommended: agent definition carries an explicit temperature (c), revisit pointer (a) only if model-default sampling is needed.

Surfaced by US1b final review. Files: internal/platform/ai/anthropic.go, openaicompat.go (Temperature wire fields), schema.go (Request.Temperature).

title: SPA renders stale logged-in dashboard on 401 (/me) instead of redirecting to /login
description: UX bug observed during 002 dogfooding (not in spec-002 tasks). When the backend rejects a cached access token (e.g. after a dev JWT keyring regen on restart), the /me call returns 401 but the SPA keeps rendering a stale 'Signed in as…' dashboard rather than redirecting to /login. Repro: cache a token, restart backend (new keyring), reload SPA. Expected: a 401 on /me clears the session and routes to /login. Workaround today: click Sign out. Likely in the web/ auth interceptor/guard. Non-blocking; surfaced in the 002 HANDOFF across multiple sessions.
close_reason: NOT REPRODUCING. Verified in real browser: expired-access+valid-refresh transparently refreshes (401 → /auth/refresh 200 → retry 200); invalid-refresh and no-token both redirect to /login; no stale dashboard. Token iat==page-load proved the 'zombie' read was benign console noise. Evidence: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-02-support-desk-usability-design.md. No code fix was needed.

title: US4 follow-up: inbox-settings single saving() signal serializes mutations
description: web/src/app/pages/support/inbox-settings.ts uses one saving() signal, so a slow per-domain Verify disables the add-domain and add-address forms too. Per-action loading signals (verify already has verifyingId) would let independent actions proceed. UX rough edge, non-blocking.

title: US4 follow-up: RSA-2048 DKIM fallback (Ed25519-only shipped)
description: T057 shipped Ed25519-only DKIM signing. research R3 specifies RSA-2048 as a fallback for receivers that don't validate Ed25519 DKIM. Add per-domain RSA-2048 keygen + signing as a fallback/secondary selector. Non-blocking; Ed25519 is widely supported and go-msgauth signs+verifies it.
design:
SCOPED 2026-06-02 (deferred to feature work, NOT polish — ~MEDIUM, multi-day).

GOAL: 'RSA-2048 fallback for receivers that don't validate Ed25519 DKIM' (research R3). The ONLY design that achieves this is DUAL-PUBLISH + DUAL-SIGN (not pick-one-per-domain): generate BOTH keys, publish TWO DNS TXT records (k=ed25519 + k=rsa), emit TWO DKIM-Signature headers so each receiver validates whichever it supports.

CHANGE MAP (file:line):
1. Keygen — internal/ticketing/identity.go:~190 CreateEmailDomain() ed25519.GenerateKey only → also rsa.GenerateKey(2048); seal both.
2. Schema — migrations/ NEW migration: add dkim_rsa_selector, dkim_rsa_public_key, dkim_rsa_private_key_ref to email_domain (+ db/schema.sql + make generate for dbgen). get_send_identity DEFINER (migration 0023) must also return the RSA columns.
3. Send path — internal/platform/notify/sender_subscriber.go:189-228 selectCustomIdentity(): line 216 len(keyBytes)!=ed25519.PrivateKeySize HARD-REJECTS non-ed25519 — must load+unseal BOTH keys; smtp.go signDKIM already crypto.Signer-agnostic (no change) but Send must sign twice (two DKIM-Signature headers) OR Mail carries []*DKIMConfig.
4. DNS publication — internal/ticketing/identity.go:~522 toEmailDomain() hardcodes 'k=ed25519' → expose a SECOND DKIMRecord for the RSA selector. API contract change.
5. OpenAPI — contracts/openapi.yaml DNSChallenge: dkim_record (single) → dkim_records (list) or add dkim_rsa_record.
6. Tests — internal/platform/notify/dkim_signing_integration_test.go (DB+infra): add RSA keygen+sign+verify; assert BOTH signatures present + cryptographically valid. Plus identity keygen test.

NOTE: system DKIM (cmd/manyforge/main.go dkimConfigFromCfg) ALREADY parses both PKCS8-ed25519 and PKCS1-RSA — custom keygen should mirror. Ed25519 is widely supported so this is genuinely non-blocking.
notes: Deferred to feature work (decision 2026-06-02). Backlog-clear pass closed the other 6 spec-002 follow-ups (9li/0yv/zzr/no9/7c0/mu7); 3jt is feature-scale (dual-publish DKIM), see design field.

title: Authenticated JSON API lacks request-body-size cap (httpx.DecodeJSON)
description: Final US3 review (T044-T051) flagged: httpx.DecodeJSON streams the request body with no http.MaxBytesReader, and the /api/v1 protected routes have no global request-size middleware (only ReadHeaderTimeout is set; InboundMaxBytes covers only webhook/SMTP). Pre-existing across US1/US2 reply/note and now US3 triage. Authenticated + permission-gated so practical DoS risk is low, but constitution input-validation/defense-in-depth calls for a per-helper or middleware body cap. Add http.MaxBytesReader in DecodeJSON (or a body-limit middleware on protected routes). NOT a US3 regression. Relates to epic manyforge-n0q.

title: US2 follow-up: outbound body_html dropped at MIME layer (text/plain only)
description: body_html is plumbed end-to-end (DB -> get_send_context -> notify.Mail.BodyHTML) but buildMIME (internal/platform/notify/smtp.go) only emits text/plain + BodyText. Harmless in US2 (composer is text-only, body_html always nil) but a latent gap: an API caller can POST body_html, see it stored/returned, yet it's never delivered. Add a multipart/alternative sender (text + html) in a follow-up. Surfaced in US2 final review.

title: US2 doc accuracy: OpenAPI/Mail comments overstate sending identity + [#ref] subject
description: The /reply OpenAPI summary says 'from the verified custom identity if present' and notify.Mail.Subject comment says it 'carries the [#<reply_token>] tag', but US2 sends from the system identity only (get_send_context joins kind='system') and the subject is plain 'Re: ...' (the [#ref] subject-token fallback is NOT built; threading rides the VERP Reply-To). Tighten the docs/comments or mark deferred. Custom-identity/DKIM = US4; [#ref] subject matching = deferred. Surfaced in US2 final review.

title: US1 test-hardening follow-ups (non-blocking)
description:
Minor test/quality items found during US1 review (all units APPROVED; these strengthen pins/coverage). Batch before final US1 review:
1. replytoken: add deterministic TestVerifyReplyTokenNonCanonicalEncoding (fixed key+uuid, padding-bit flips on BOTH id and sig segments) — current flipped-last-byte case only exercises the canonical-base64 malleability defense ~6.25% of runs (threading_idempotency_test.go:46).
2. SMTP: add multi-RCPT fan-out test (two accepted recipients in one DATA → assert two ingests w/ correct per-recipient EnvelopeRecipient); smtp.go:253 loop currently untested for fan-out.
3. webhook: add a test for the sha256= prefix-strip path in verify() (webhook.go:77) — currently dead/untested; or remove the tolerance (YAGNI, contract is bare hex).
4. webhook: clarifying comment on remoteIP() (X-Forwarded-For intentionally ignored — RemoteAddr only, not trusted for routing/auth).
5. smtp_integration_test port reservation: one-line comment that close-then-rebind is inherently racy (test-only).
Found across f2339ec/0eff959/f37d776.

title: Investigate Cursor subscription credentials for opencode reviews (feasibility spike — INADVISABLE)
description:
## Goal (as requested)
Explore letting a manyforge user run opencode reviews on their **Cursor subscription**
model quota.

## Is it possible? — Technically Moderate to stand up, but STRATEGICALLY INADVISABLE.
Recommendation: do NOT build as product infrastructure. Treat this epic as a timeboxed
feasibility spike whose default outcome is a documented NO-GO + "revisit only if Cursor
ships an official model API."

## Why
- No official raw-model / chat-completion API. Cursor's official surfaces (Admin,
  Analytics, AI Code Tracking, Bugbot, Cloud Agents, TS/Python agent SDKs) are
  agent/admin-level and key-based; NONE is a completion endpoint. BYOK is the inverse of
  what we want (you bring your OWN provider key, and Agent/Composer stay locked to
  Cursor's backend anyway).
- The ONLY way to spend Cursor subscription quota outside the editor is to impersonate
  the Cursor client: reverse-engineered Connect-RPC/protobuf over HTTP/2 against
  api2.cursor.sh (/aiserver.v1.ChatService/StreamUnifiedChatWithTools), forging
  `x-cursor-checksum` (a time-based "jyh" XOR cipher over timestamp + machineId),
  pinning a current `x-cursor-client-version`, and shipping .proto schemas scraped from
  Cursor's obfuscated VSCode-fork JS. In practice: run a local cursor->OpenAI-compat
  transcoding proxy inside the sandbox and point opencode at it.
- ToS (Anysphere) directly prohibits it: 1.5(i) no reverse-engineering, 1.5(viii) no
  scraping, 17.1 no transfer of rights; 9 lets them suspend/terminate with no refund.
  Cursor already hard-routes Agent/Edit through its billing backend and has locked out
  BYOK users.
- The credential itself is trivially reusable (WorkOS session JWT
  `WorkosCursorSessionToken` = user_id::JWT, read from state.vscdb / a browser cookie) —
  so the token side is easy; it's the impersonation shell around it that's fragile.

## The manyforge multiplier
manyforge cloud reviews are multi-tenant product infra (GitHub App auto-review is live
for multiple orgs). Piping many orgs' reviews through impersonated Cursor sessions is a
ban magnet with constant breakage — checksum, client-version, and protobuf all rotate
every Cursor release, and upstream maintainers list "auto-update when Cursor releases" as
an UNSOLVED TODO. This is a strictly worse path than the Codex epic, which at least has
official opencode support.
design:
## Recommended disposition
1. Spike (timeboxed): against a THROWAWAY Cursor account, confirm the current
   api2.cursor.sh protocol + jyh checksum + client-version pinning still work, and
   measure how quickly they break across Cursor releases. Output = a go/no-go memo.
2. Default recommendation: NO-GO as product infrastructure. Close in favor of one of:
   - waiting for an official Cursor model API, or
   - the Codex epic (official opencode support, the sanctioned analogue), or
   - plain BYOK — the user brings their own OpenAI/Anthropic key, already supported today.

## IF a spike ever greenlights it (NOT recommended), it would need
- The same net-new OAuth/token machinery as the Codex epic (no static-key fit): sealed
  token storage, refresh, per-run injection.
- A cursor->OpenAI-compat transcoding proxy bundled into the sandbox image: protobuf
  schemas, the jyh checksum, machineId (storage.serviceMachineId), a pinned+auto-updated
  client version, and Connect envelope framing [type:1][len:4BE][payload].
- Egress allowlist entries for api2.cursor.sh + api.workos.com; a client-version
  auto-updater to survive Cursor releases.
- Explicit acceptance of: zero support, a clear ToS violation, per-release breakage, and
  account-ban exposure for the user whose subscription is used.

## Top risks
1. Account termination (ToS 1.5(i)/(viii)/17.1 + 9; Cursor blocks off-client routing and
   has banned BYOK abusers; checksum/machineId/client-version are a ready fingerprint).
2. Constant breakage / high maintenance — checksum, client-version, and scraped protobuf
   change every release; upstream flags auto-update as unsolved.
3. Legal/contractual exposure from the reverse-engineering + scraping clauses; no SLA,
   no official surface, and upstream providers' own abuse filters can catch the traffic.

## References
eisbaw/cursor_api_demo, everestmz/cursor-rpc (Go), wisdgod/cursor-api; Cursor ToS + MSA;
WorkOS AuthKit sessions; apidog BYOK-ban writeup. Full research captured in this session.

title: manyforge-7lx polish: among-tail dedup, earlier-err/later-live test, unmarshal debug log
description: Low-priority follow-ups from the manyforge-7lx (N-fallback chains) final whole-branch review, all reviewer-approved as non-blocking: (1) reviewLane's runtime dedup guard compares tail entries only against the chosen lane, not among each other — duplicate NON-chosen chain entries each burn one doomed sandbox run (not a correctness bug; still returns first success/original error). (2) Add a unit test for a chain where an EARLIER candidate is unresolvable (err) and a LATER one is live — algorithm handles it (firstResolvableIdx), currently only hand-verified. (3) Add a slog.Debug on json.Unmarshal error in the fallback_chain read path (panel.go, review_config_service.go) — currently best-effort _=Unmarshal; malformed degrades to empty chain (recorded skip, never a misroute). Chain-length cap and the stale laneRest comment were fixed pre-merge, not here.

title: 008 Slice 4 — Later: risk-tier router, per-repo overrides, parallel fan-out
description: Cloudflare-style LLM risk-tier triage (trivial/lite/full depth scaling). Per-repo dimension overrides. Parallel cloud fan-out. Cross-iteration already-fixed tracking. Structured rule-ID index.

title: Connector failed-op dismiss/retry (degraded health sticks after transient failures)
description: healthState() = failedOps>0 -> degraded, and there is no UI/API to dismiss or retry a failed connector_outbound_op. So a single transient failure (e.g. 'jira: service unreachable') leaves attempts exhausted -> permanently 'failed' -> connector shows 'degraded' forever even after connectivity recovers (Test passes). Add a way to retry (re-enqueue to pending) or dismiss/acknowledge failed ops from the connectors UI/API, so health reflects current reality. Surfaced live 2026-06-14 (3 stale test-junk failed comment ops kept the connector degraded; cleared manually via SQL).
notes: DONE 2026-06-19: driver #1 shipped. Migration 0066 adds 'dismissed' enum; RetryFailedOps (failed->pending, attempts reset) + DismissFailedOps (failed->dismissed) service methods + POST /connectors/{cid}/failed-ops/{retry,dismiss} endpoints + 004 openapi. Connectors UI shows Retry/Dismiss when failed_outbound_ops>0. Verified end-to-end in real browser (degraded->healthy after dismiss). Backend 8f21b92, frontend de1febf.

title: Support page should seed CurrentBusinessService so the approvals nav badge tracks the viewed business
description: Final review of Stream 2 (manyforge-4zs.2) noted: only the /approvals page calls CurrentBusinessService.set(); ticket-list.ts keeps its own businessId signal and never seeds the shared service. A user working only in Support who selects a non-default business gets an approvals nav badge reflecting items[0] (or blank) rather than the business they're viewing. Matches the documented best-effort design, but tightening it (have Support seed the same CurrentBusinessService) would make the badge accurate everywhere. Low priority. Files: web/src/app/pages/support/ticket-list.ts, web/src/app/core/current-business.service.ts.

title: Add dedicated connector write-tool RBAC-denied behavioral test (connectors.write:false)
description: Opus US6 review nice-to-have (non-blocking): runner_test.go covers RBAC-deny generically (errResolver on a non-connector tool) + the MF-004-US6 perm source pins, but lacks a dedicated behavioral case asserting add_external_comment/transition_external_status are denied (no enqueue, no approval) when the agent's perms lack connectors.write. Belt-and-suspenders.

title: Remove or repurpose unused TopicConnectorOutboundSync const
description: connector.go declares TopicConnectorOutboundSync (from US2) but US4 routes outbound via the connector_outbound_op poller, so the const is referenced nowhere. Either delete it or repurpose as a low-latency dispatcher wakeup signal. Trivial cleanup, noted in plan D1 + US4 close review.

title: When UpdateConnectorCredential is added, it MUST re-validate + re-audit allow_private_base_url
description: US1 ships create-time validation+audit of the on-prem trust flag only. A future connector credential/base_url update path must re-run validateBaseURL AND re-audit the trust grant (create-time guard alone is insufficient once base_url/trust become mutable). Mirrors spec-003 deo.11.

title: When an UpdateAIProviderCredential query is added, it MUST carry allow_private_base_url
description: Surfaced in US8 review: db/query/ai.sql has no UPDATE query today. When one is added, it must include allow_private_base_url (and re-validate it via validateBaseURL + re-audit a trust grant) or an update will silently zero the flag. Reminder issue.

title: netsafe: guard against empty LookupIPAddr result before ips[0] dial
description: Pre-existing (surfaced in US8 review): netsafe NewClientWithOptions DialContext indexes ips[0] after LookupIPAddr without checking len(ips)>0. LookupIPAddr normally errors when no addresses are found, but the guarantee is soft; an empty non-error slice would panic. Add a len(ips)==0 -> error guard. internal/platform/netsafe/client.go.

title: US7 follow-up: parameterize run_cursor + clampRunLimit doc + OpenAPI limit min/max
description: Code-quality nits from US7 reviews: (1) run_cursor.go could adopt the parameterized encodeCursor(kind,keyset) core like internal/ticketing/cursor.go before a 2nd agents list endpoint; the page-1 sentinel (9999-01-01 + all-Fs UUID) are magic values worth naming. (2) clampRunLimit lacks the DoS-cap doc comment that ticketing.clampLimit has. (3) OpenAPI 'limit' param should document default(50)/max(100).

title: US7 follow-up: date-only custom window midnight truncation + budget_pct UI hint
description: Design §6 known: a custom window to=YYYY-MM-DD parses to UTC midnight and the half-open [from,to) excludes that whole day. The API/OpenAPI accept 'custom' even though the UI never sends it, so an API caller can hit this. Options: normalize 'to' to end-of-day, or document clearly. Also surface a 'budget % shown only for this month' hint in the accounting UI. From US7 final review.

title: US7 follow-up: remove redundant dead ListAgentRunsByAgent query
description: db/query/agent_run.sql has an older unbounded ListAgentRunsByAgent (LIMIT $3) that predates US7 (US3 commit 26abcc2) and is never called from hand-written code. US7's keyset ListAgentRuns supersedes it. Remove + regenerate sqlc. Pre-existing, not a US7 regression.

title: US7 follow-up: test LoadModelRegistry empty-catalog fail-loud path
description: model_pricing_test.go covers the WithTx-error path but not the reg.Len()==0 -> error branch (the 'fail loudly on unseeded catalog' invariant). Add a unit test where WithTx succeeds but returns zero rows. From US7 final review.

title: US6 follow-up: legacy SSE transport fallback for MCP
description: v1 targets Streamable-HTTP (2025-06-18). Some MCP servers may only speak the legacy HTTP+SSE transport. Add a fallback path if a target server requires it. Spec 003 US6 §6.

title: US6 follow-up: MCP OAuth / richer auth schemes
description: v1 sealed_auth_ref supports only {scheme:bearer,token}. Add OAuth (client-credentials / authorization-code) and other auth schemes for MCP servers. Extend the sealed blob shape + connect flow. Spec 003 US6 §6.

title: US6 follow-up: tools/list caching across runs
description: v1 connects to each opted-in MCP server and tools/list's at every run start (per-run connection lifecycle). Cache discovered tool defs per server with a TTL/invalidation to cut per-run latency + load on remote servers. Perf optimization. Spec 003 US6 §5.

title: US3 nit: validate agent_run target_type + document target_id as hint-not-gate
description: Holistic review (US3 close-out): agent_run.target_type is free-text (no CHECK) while trigger has a CHECK — add a CHECK or service-layer allowlist {'ticket'} for consistency (blast radius is nil today: parameterized SQL + string-compare only). Also add a one-line comment at RunService.Trigger noting target_id is a prompt HINT, not an ownership gate (tools are the authorization boundary under the agent's RLS) so a future reader doesn't mistake it for a missed ownership check.

title: US3 follow-up: share permission-key constants between tool registry, ticketing handlers, and migrations
description: internal/agents/tools.go hardcodes RequiredPerm string literals (tickets.read/write/assign/reply); internal/ticketing/handler.go and the 0015 migration use the same bare strings. If the permission catalog ever defines exported Perm* constants, these call sites should reference them so a rename fails at compile time rather than silently mis-gating. Low risk (perms are migration-seeded). Surfaced in US3 Task 4 code-quality review (M2).

title: knownProviders set should track the ai_provider PG enum
description: internal/agents/credential.go knownProviders is an untyped map[string]bool that must stay in lockstep with the ai_provider Postgres enum (migration 0025) + dbgen.AiProvider* constants. Convert to dbgen.AiProvider* constants or a generated set when the enum next changes (e.g. adding a provider). Flagged in US1a final review; non-blocking. Part of Spec 003 (manyforge-deo).

title: US4 follow-up: DKIM master-key base64/hex parse disambiguation
description: internal/platform/config envKey32 prefers the decode yielding exactly 32 bytes; a 64-char all-hex-alphabet base64 string is taken as a 32-byte hex key. Cannot arise from a correctly-encoded 32-byte key (44-char base64 or 64-char hex are unambiguous). Optional hardening: require explicit base64:/hex: prefix or anchor hex on ^[0-9a-fA-F]{64}$. Nit.

Concurrency regression tests for 'HTTP fetch held across a mutex' bugs (e.g. OpenRouterModels/HuggingFaceModels ensure()) CANNOT be pinned with a wall-clock assertion. With a cold cache the buggy locked path also finishes in ~1*latency: the first caller fetches under the lock (warming the cache) and callers 2..N block on the mutex, then short-circuit on the freshness check — only ONE fetch happens. The fixed path finishes in the same elapsed time but with up to N overlapping fetches. Elapsed time is identical; only the fetch COUNT differs, and permitting hits==1 (as the HF/OpenRouter timing tests did) means the test does not catch the regression at all. Correct approach = deterministic barrier: each fake-server fetch parks until >=2 are in-flight at once, assert maxInflight>=2. Unlocked → all callers reach the barrier together, releases instantly; under the mutex → serialized, max in-flight stays 1, guard timeout trips failure. Proven in internal/agents/openrouter_models_test.go TestOpenRouterModels_ConcurrentCallersDoNotSerializeOnTheFetch (manyforge-9v9, PR #44): FAILs (max=1, 2.0s) with fetch under lock, PASSes (0.02s) without. The HF sibling test still has the old blind spot → manyforge-r0z4.

OpenAI's ChatGPT auth (auth.openai.com) has NO OAuth device-code grant — its OIDC discovery advertises only authorization_code + refresh_token, and POST /oauth/device/code returns a Cloudflare 403 (verified from inside the hub cluster). The official codex CLI (openai/codex, codex-rs/login) uses only authorization_code + PKCE with a fixed http://localhost:1455/auth/callback loopback redirect. A hosted web app therefore MUST use the paste-the-redirect-URL flow (/oauth/authorize + /oauth/token, the CLI's endpoints) — there is no server-side device flow. Do not reintroduce one (pinned in internal/security_regression/codex_oauth_pin_test.go). CRM/inbox principal-less constraint: the inbound-email path runs under WithTx with no principal GUC, so RLS WITH CHECK blocks writes to tenant-wide RLS tables (contact/company). Writes from that path MUST go through a SECURITY DEFINER SQL function (runs as table owner, bypasses non-FORCE RLS) — e.g. crm_link_inbound_sender (migration 0059), like ingest_inbound_message. The Go ContactService.ResolveOrCreate* methods can't be used there. Relevant for Phase B activity-recording hooks. Also: adding a param to a Postgres function via CREATE OR REPLACE creates an OVERLOAD not a replace — use a separate function or DROP+CREATE.

Adding an AI provider to manyforge that runs in the opencode review SANDBOX means clearing TWO prod-only gates beyond the app code, both easy to miss because dev/chart defaults hide them: (1) EGRESS — the Flux HelmRelease override at infra-k8s flux/clusters/proxmox-talos/manyforge.yaml 'sandbox.egressAllow' is a hand-maintained list (local vLLM/ollama IPs) that does NOT inherit charts/manyforge/values.yaml's default, so a new provider host (e.g. chatgpt.com for openai_codex) must be added THERE too or every lane fails the enqueue pre-flight 'provider host X not in sandbox egress allowlist' and silently falls back; (2) LIVENESS PROBER — internal/agents/coding/prober.go does a generic GET {base_url}/models with only a Bearer header; a backend needing extra auth headers (codex: ChatGPT-Account-Id+originator+versioned UA, 403s a bare GET) is always marked 'not live' and skipped. Fix pattern: assume-live in the prober (like anthropic) when the provider has no cheap unauthenticated probe AND its validity is enforced at resolve time. Symptom of either gate: dimension configured for provider X but dimension_runs shows the fallback provider; grep app logs for 'not in sandbox egress allowlist' (gate 1) vs 'lane candidate not live ... provider' (gate 2).

bd (beads) in manyforge uses an embedded Dolt store as source of truth; .beads/issues.jsonl is an EXPORT that can lag. 'bd close/update' mutates Dolt immediately (bd show reflects it) but does NOT always rewrite issues.jsonl, so 'git add .beads/ && commit' can silently commit stale (still-open) state — the close then isn't durable across clones/sessions. ALWAYS run 'bd export -o .beads/issues.jsonl' (or 'bd export') right before git add/commit of bead changes, and verify the id's status in the committed jsonl (git show HEAD:.beads/issues.jsonl | grep <id>). Observed 2026-07-22: 3n8l/ost5 closes stuck in Dolt while jsonl still showed 'open'.

sqlc.embed + folded aggregates: COALESCE((SELECT array_agg(x) ...), '{}') infers Go type interface{}; move the cast OUTSIDE so the column is unambiguously text[] -> []string: COALESCE((...), '{}')::text[] AS tags. Also: changing a plpgsql function's RETURN TABLE columns or args needs DROP FUNCTION + CREATE in the migration (CREATE OR REPLACE cannot alter return type/signature) — keep a down migration restoring the prior signature. See migrations/0024 (manyforge-axq) + db/query/ticketing.sql.

This repo has NO bd dolt remote configured, so 'bd dolt push' is a no-op. bd issue state travels via .beads/issues.jsonl committed into git. The bd hook auto-stages that journal, which makes 'git pull --rebase' error 'cannot pull with rebase: unstaged changes' — harmless when origin isn't ahead (verify with: git log origin/master..HEAD). After 'bd close <id>' when everything else is already committed, make a dedicated 'chore(bd): close <id>' commit, then git push.
